text:
Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Best guy this side of the golden gate bridge

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC101 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Prof Souza is amazing. I was terrified to take CSC and had a lot of preconceived notions about taking CSC, but all of those fears were shattered by how great a Prof he is. Every topic was presented in an understandable and digestible way. He treats every student with respect and truly wants to see you succeed. Def take his class.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Great professor. Took it in summer, lectures mainly focused on assignments and had recordings. Get ready to read, instructions of assignments are very long but not difficult. Always turned the lecture before assignment deadline into an optional lab session. Attendance as EC and also provided EC on assignments.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
He is chill and understanding. His lectures are amazing. He makes us understand the materials easily. Take his class, you will not regret.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 1.0 | Difficulty: 5.0
Don't take him unless you're unemployed and/or enrolled part-tine. That is where all his good reviews come from. Assignments have prompts up to 20 pages long, or more. And he doesn't prepare you for them at all. Allergic to teaching. Expects students to learn everything on their own. Does not approve W's or pass/no-pass. Hates his job and students.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 3.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
This course is more about how much you put in. The class lectures were middle of the ground, most of the time it feels like hes just trying to take up time till he hits the end of class. Yeah its a easy course basically a free A. If you do everything and dont use AI you learn quite a bit, but can't say its due to him more so due to the projects.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Took his class over the summer, so it was a bit more accelerated, but the way he taught the material was good and I enjoyed his class overall. It was a solo project which I heard has been turned into a group project, so it was a bit more difficult but he was pretty lenient with the grading.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 1.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
I don't understand his high rating. He gets very off-topic. I had to watch recordings of lectures even though I attended them. Applying the lectures to the homework is like searching for a needle in a haystack he is so off-topic. Also, I had a very serious injury preventing me from taking the final. He ghosted and failed me like a coward. Unreal!!!

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC101 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Loved his csc101, felt like rather than straight up zombie coding I was actually learning something, it felt good and he was always there to answer questions.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC101 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Good Professor

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CS210 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 5.0
Best cs teacher ever

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: 13327 | Quality: 2.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
The only reason I got through are because of the DL small work groups, being talked to for an hour without being able to do hands on work was just not a good way for myself to be introduced to computer science.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CS210 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
There is not much else to say but he's a good professor

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Overall great professor. Graded mainly on three assignments and the final one is a bit tricky, but he gives lots of help in lectures and curves at the end.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: 13327 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Amazing professor.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Professor Souza is the best teacher at SFSU in my opinion. He knows the material and how to teach. Attendance isn't required, but make sure to go to class, or you'll fall behind.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CS210 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
He is alright.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CS210 | Quality: 4.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
He is the greatest teacher in the world.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
overall caring person. has to be one of the top professors at SFSU. thanks for a great semester .

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
spent the semester working on a project and it was pretty fun but got a little too hand-holdy near the end, quizzes weren't too bad with 2-3 attempts, due dates were fairly flexible with various extensions provided

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
There are 3 assignments-Calculator, Interpreter, Tank Game(final+presentation).The assignments are difficult but he gives partial credit and extends by a few days.Many people are worried about the tank game but he provides starter code. Also for the presentation some people presented broken code so there isn't anything to worry about :) (fall 2022)

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Professor Souza is the best teacher I have ever had, he cares a lot about students and helps a lot with assignments and even with the final project he will guide you through it. He knows a lot about coding and explains the topics very well.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Professor Souza will go out of his way to help you get your quizzes and assignments turned in on time. We have a term project and he helps you a LOT throughout. Attendance is not mandatory as he records each lecture and posts it on canvas.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
Professor souza is knowledgable and cares about his students

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
He basically holds your hand during the semester. Always there for support & extensions if someone needs it. I really appreciate the fact that he uploads his lectures (especially with covid/monkeypox cases on the rise). The way he uses Discord for participation is clever and convenient! Do the Milestones on time & the project will be less stressful

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
This man is an absolute gem. Makes it very clear what he expects of you in for each and every assignment with quizzes that relate directly back to the slides and lectures videos that he makes available. Is somewhat lecture heavy but thats standard for CS classes in the semester system. (He was my professor him for CSC 413 as well.)

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
Chill dude. He's kind, caring, extends most of his assignment's deadline by a few days. But DON'T start on your HWs super close to the deadline or else you're gonna have a bad time. Most of them are decently challenging, but not as difficult as other CSC413 classes. Quality > Quantity class in terms of workload. The Tank Game project was fun to do.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
I've been taking all classes taught by Souza; incredibly caring, understanding and straight forward in the class. Most of the deadlines he extends a couple of days. Don't slack off, and put effort into it; it's not hard to get an A if you start early, and understand the materials. Recommend buying Jon Duckett books; its good for this class.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 4.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Professor Souza created probably one of the best online formats for the pandemic so far. The asynchronistic nature of his lecture set up means its entirely on you to figure out how you want to approach the class, however going through each video one by one you'll have a great understanding on how to tackle the projects given.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
I would just say the best Professor I have ever had Very helpful in you r hard times Very understandable I wish he teaches all cs classes I would always take him

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
Ramps up heavily near the end of the semester, don't procrastinate. Do the readings as soon as you can and really try to understand the subject. Build little projects. For the final project you can complete it using the lecture alone but it will be time consuming. You can also complete the final project using your own research in a faster manner.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
His quizzes are fairly easy as long as you do the readings. The assignments however, I would say they're quite difficult since I'm not really good with programming. If you at least put in the effort in his class and asks questions, you will definitely pass. Don't start your assignment at the last minute though, or else you will regret it.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Definitely enjoy his 413 a lot more during summer 2021 time. Anthony himself was the best teacher aside from 2 of my other professors whom I previously had. Would highly recommend those that want to know CSC 317 and 413 to take his class. summertime is the best time. Wish he teaches CSC 415 OS class.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
By far one of my favorite professors at SFSU. He makes it very clear to his students what he expects from them and there are never any surprises. He is a fair grader and does a good job at explaining what we need work on. I took him over the summer and I was able to learn a lot in such a short period of time.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Clearly outlines what he wants for the assignments. Provides a lot of resources so that you can complete all of your assignments with A's.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Best professor hands down! really caring for students and highly knowledgeable. makes class fun and enjoyable where students can learn, office hours are tremendously helpful. 4 assignments and easy quizzes with one final project which is covered in extreme detail and FUN. Would definitely take again!

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
One of my favorite professors. Super chill about deadlines, just put in some effort and he will pass you

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
The most patient and organized professor. He is a real gem.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Intro to web dev based on 4 assignments, quizzes, and one project. Quizzes can be retaken. Very helpful in office hours.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
One of my most favorite professors ever. Anthony really cares for his students. He extended the deadline for the final project twice so students would have the time to catch up and get a good grade. I wish he taught upper division courses :( Would definitely take him again if I could. Highly recommended!

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Incredible man. Class was asynchronous, posted videos and slides in a timely matter. Class was pretty straight forward. Had 8 quizzes based on material in videos and slides. He explained homework assignments very detailed, which helped a lot. Definitely recommend <3

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Has weekly quizzes based on lectures. Has three assignments, one for HTML, one for CSS, and one for javascript, which all lead up to a term project. He gives you a month to do the term project and helps you do a lot of it in a way where it's manageable and you're able to learn a lot. He's also very accessible through email, discord, and slack!

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
If you are a beginner on web development, take this class. He is really good with his lectures. His notes are straightforward and match the quizzes really well. His assignments are great. He guides you step by step on how to do them. Web development has so much interesting material. I wish they could expanding the knowledge of JavaScript.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 4.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Professor haas experience in the industry which is always nice. The video lectures are straightforward and incredibly useful. Class gets fairly hard by assignment 4. The final project is pretty involved, make sure you give yourself time to work on it. Focus on quality in the first 3 assignments and final becomes way easier.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC415 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Good professor. He is an easy grader and as long as you try in the class, you won't fail. All the quizzes were fairly easy if you do the reading. The final wasn't too hard either since some of the questions come from past quizzes. Been taking him for 317, 413, and 415!

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Very knowledgeable with the material. Lectures were informative and assigns a project to see what you have learned. Has office to help anybody who has trouble with starting the project. Everything in the final is covered in the lect. Pay attention to his lectures, make sure you know his grading criteria, and ask him question during his office hours

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC415 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
He is absolutely the best professor at SFSU. He use to be student and he knows the real game. He give you assignments that you can actually have ability to do it not some random things to make you fail. He understands the struggles he is not there to make you fail the class. I have been taking him for 317, 413, 415 he is amazing. take it !

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Souza was an excellent professor and I really enjoyed his teaching style. As a result of COVID, we switched to per-recorded (live coding) lectures which made it easy to follow the class. Quizes were based heavily on the slides, make sure to study them. If you're serious about programming, this class will be a breeze. Would 100% take him again.

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC413 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
He is really caring and makes you work hard! Go to his office hours and start his projects early and you will pass!

Professor: Anthony Souza | Course: CSC317 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Just gotta say I had a hard time because he's too chill and I'm not that productive while on quarantine. I smashed final project within one month, it was stupid idea but he was nice.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Can't wait to take the next 2 courses with Professor Ta! The only professor who is always positive and productive, even during this sad time at State U, despite continued destructive budget cuts and historic admission declines. His courses are still the best. He works super hard. He has a HUGE heart for his students, and he is a gifted educator.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Work hard, and you will find this professor the best. He cares that you learn and be ready for the demands in the workplace. Always start assignments early and pay attention during lecture. You will enjoy his teaching thoroughly. He is a natural and genuine educator. His materials are enormously useful.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
Uses examples with mickey mouse. Idk what to tell you. its fun. Joke aside, he goes greatly in depth about things that other professors will just gloss over. Stuff professors would allocate 1 week to he does 2. He wants to make sure you actually understand how something works instead of just telling you how to use it.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
He is the best computer science professor in SFSU. He gives you a lot of opportunities to boost your grade during the semester. His explanations are very clear.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
He is the best professors I have ever taken in my life. He teaches really well and gives you a lot of opportunities to boost your grade.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
The best educator in the entire history of education! TAKE HIM!!!

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Funny guy, good at making lectures engaging. Great at explaining concepts in depth. For homework, though, ALWAYS start early. You will be crying if you don't. For 220, he scares you with a huge first assignment to weed out quitters. He ensures that he always goes at a pace that the whole class is comfortable with and will answer any questions.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
My man. He makes learning so fun and effective. He is definitely the best.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Extremely lucky to have had him for my last 3 courses. I do not believe I will find a better professor than Professor Ta in my life. Extremely grateful. He is a gifted educator who works hard and loves his students. He genuinely cares and possesses endless patience. He is a gem at SFSU.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Professor Ta designed his courses thoroughly and properly. He makes proper adjustments along the way. This takes hard work, passion, experience, and a big heart. Not to mention his natural outstanding ability to teach so effectively, which by itself alone already sets him superior to any other professors at State U. Hats off.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
He is very kind, and he can really teach. He structures this course in a way that best benefits students. Regardless of how hectic this fall semester has been, this course has already managed a massive review, lots of good practices every lecture, and good projects. Professor taught all topics carefully. He taught the Google data structures too.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
His approach is unique. Cant copy his style because he is adaptive to each student. It was so fun to watch him come up with personalized examples right on the spot to help students understand the most abstract and difficult concepts in OOP. He cares. He is passionate. He is so experienced and skillful at teaching. He works hard. Lucky to have him.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 5.0
Fortunately enrolled in his CSC 340 but would wait a semester or a year if I have to. This professor is magical. He knows how to teach the dumbest, like me, to love the subject. I was ready to quit but now doing well. I am grateful to him. His courses are fantastic. Better work hard even though he pressures noone never. He shows the path. U choose.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
His 340 beats the other 340 in the department by far. Intense but always fun learning. Advanced materials. He prepares you to compete for the demanding jobs. A big picture guy. He does not waste your or his time on the typical petty classroom issues. He is a great educator. Unique and hyper effective teaching style. He has a charming personality.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
The best students in the department take his courses. He never cuts corners. He starts the semester with a huge review. He covers all topics and the additional ones. All topics are taught carefully and discussed in much detail and great depth. He provides a wide range of support. Prof. Ta is phenomenal in teaching and management. Engaging lectures.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
I appreciate how he cares for students' future rather than just a passing grade for the course. He tries every lecture to transfer the knowledge, teach problem-solving techniques, and train us on how to think. His simple life stories are encouraging and calling. I like how he can explain both difficult CS concepts and abstract life matters. Best.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
I learned a great deal this semester in his 215 class. Enrolled in his 220 for spring. I will take all of his courses. His lectures are so good. He can explain difficult concepts and make them fun to absorb. His reviews are helpful and take us to advanced learning. His packages and the file manager are better than any textbooks.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Prof. Ta is stellar, but never stops trying to give more to his students and the community at State U. I have known him for a long time. Both my kids and I took his courses here. Most professors only deliver the lecture. Professor Ta cares about the learning, individual development, and future of each student. Not possible without a heart. Respect.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 2.0 | Difficulty: 5.0
Most lectures go on about him guilt tripping the class about budget cuts. Course starts with a massive grueling culling assignment that made i think 75% of the class drop or move sections. Then afterwards the assignments became baby easy with constant delays after deadlines. I seriously dont understand why this guy has as high ratings as he does.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Best of the best. He is knowledgeable. He is so effective at getting knowledge straight to your head. He created a lot of good materials and gives them FREE to students. The packages are better than textbooks. He can come up with new and interesting examples on the spot. He wont move on until you understand. His patience is endless. He is genuine.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
He was amazing from the first to the last day of the semester. He has endless patience and so much of energy and positivity. He always has a smile ready for students. He created so many opportunities to encourage and support students. His teaching is unmatched. I believe he is the best because he loves what he does and he is simply gifted. Amazing!

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
A professor who is good at what he is doing, loves what he is doing, and cares about what he is doing is Professor Duc Ta. He is gold standard. It was amazing to be his student for my last 3 courses 215, 220, and 340. I wish he had more time to teach more courses in this department. This department needs him. But he is doing a lot. The Best EVER!!!

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
He is gifted. It is hard to find a teacher who is so good at teaching and connecting to students like he is. He is very patient with students. He gives many recaps and reviews. He has a good example for anything. His examples are fun. They help students learn in the most natural way.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
I took his class this Summer. He is the best professor in SFSU. His lectures are amazing. He is very helpful and understanding. He inspires us a lot. You will be amazed by his teaching style and how amazing he is.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
His summer 220 was so good. He works extremely hard and helped us learn a great amount. He gives students no pressure but motivation and guidance. Be ready work or simply just give it up and find a better time when you can give learning your priority. He is always there to support. He gives free education. Kind, casual, intelligent he is.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Take him! He is the best of the best. You will learn a lot from him even in the summer. Professor is an outstanding educator and leader. He is inspiring. He does not just teach. He reaches out to every student. He has students' best interests at heart. He works extremely hard. His approach is effective and relatable.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Just finished 340 this summer with him. I wish I had been able to take all of his courses. Professor Ta's 340 course was the best thing happened in my student life. He is amazing at teaching. Time goes by so fast in his class, lots of information but lots of fun. He wants students to succeed. He fights for student success. *hats off to him

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
The best human being ever lived on earth. My man 100%.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Taking this course with him in summer. He is no double the best professor I ever had. Knowledgeable but approachable and personable. His teaching is easy to understand but goes deep and hits home. He is so organized and always focusing on what is best for students. He makes it possible to every student to succeed even in the summer. He cares.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC340 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Spring, Summer, and Fall. His courses are always the best. Supportive, resourceful, well-designed projects, and energetic + engaging lecture.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Professional educator and kind human being. His courses are ZERO cost. He give his book for free to all students. He save them in convenient PDF format files so students can take notes on their devices. They are labeled packages. His lecture is super engaging.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Outstanding is an understatement. Professor Duc Ta is so much loved by students for many reasons. Thanks!

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Not just a teacher and boss, Professor Ta is an educator and leader. He brings the best out of each student and empowers them to succeed.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
His lecture is better than those in private universities. His large lecture teaching is better than those pricey private tutoring services. It is nothing less than a fortune to have him at State U.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
His patience is endless. 6 weeks of reviews at the beginning of the semester. Always willing and happy to answer any question then invites more questions. He always checks in to make sure everyone understands before moving on. So many reviews and had no problems repeating a same package for 4 times. He is very sincere, professional, and respectful.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Professor works tirelessly and is always willing to repeat or change his explanation until every student understands. Appreciate his hard work. He always gives a review before starting his lecture.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Simply amazing! He is teaching a huge lecture course. Many students were not prepared properly for college and probably should not major in computer science, but Professor Ta works so hard to make it possible for all students. Hats off!

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC220 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
Outstanding educator with heart and integrity!

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
He is young but wise. He knows how to maneuver thorough the most difficult times. He always put students first and is willing to do everything within his power for students. Amazing professor and human being. Most engaging lectures. Respect.

Professor: Duc Ta | Course: CSC215 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 2.0
The best professor. He cares and he can.

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC510 | Quality: 4.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
Jose is great. This class was a bit stressful, BUT it is not hard, I probably could've done a lot better if I had better attendance. My advice is, SHOW UP, and take notes, just follow what he does on the board, even if you don't fully understand what's happening, that's all you'll need for the exams.

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC510 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
I took Algorithms with him this semester and honestly he's the best. Pros: super caring, really nice, always willing to help students. Cons: definitely not an easy A. He explains things really well and he adjusts the pace depending on how we're doing. I'm taking his Databases and Software Engineering classes next semester.

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC675 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
One of the best professors I've taken. If you're just trying to get an easy A, don't take this class. But if you actually wanna learn how databases work and how to design them the right way, this is the class. There's 5 milestones, a midterm, a final, and he gives a lot of extra credit chances.

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC675 | Quality: 1.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Worst professor ive ever had.

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC510 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
You get used to the accent. Amazing professor genuinely cares about his students. I took it over the summer, he was very leniant with the grading. Yeah the material can get complicated but he does a good job at explaining and is ready to answer questions without being dismissive. The best CS professor I had at this school. Can't say anything less!

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC510 | Quality: 2.0 | Difficulty: 1.0
Difficult to understand, and not just because of his thick accent. He has no lecture plans and just confusingly rambles on about a different algorithm each class. There is only ONE assignment, and the instructions seem to be entirely written with AI. He's really nice and accessible, but unfortunately a bad teacher.

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC675 | Quality: 4.0 | Difficulty: 4.0
Outside of the thick accent, Jose is a great teacher. 675 was a TOUGH TOUGH class but he taught it really well and you leave feeling like you learned a lot. Make sure you study for the midterms as they are a huge chunk of your grade, and he gives you opportunity to replace a lower one with a higher one. Also, don't procrastinate the project.

Professor: Jose Ortiz-Costa | Course: CSC510 | Quality: 5.0 | Difficulty: 3.0
Jose is great professor, but it was hard to understand topics in class. 3 midterms 15% each and 30% Final(True/False). Participation is 10%. He don't take attendance in every class but If you don't attend extra credit classes which he takes randomly you will loose point on participation. 5% curve after final grade. Extra Credit Opportunities!!!
